title: Arrays, Dynamic Sequences, and Locality · Structure Brief: Two Pointer
summary: Design an event buffer with predictable indexing, insertion, and memory behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa two pointer increment in an array-backed sequence with operation traces. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain explicit two-pointer invariants for partition and interval problems.
Compare indexed access, search, middle insertion, deletion, slicing, and copying costs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
def insertion_sort(values: list[int]) -> list[int]:
    result = values.copy()
    for end in range(1, len(result)):
        key = result[end]
        cursor = end - 1
        while cursor >= 0 and result[cursor] > key:
            result[cursor + 1] = result[cursor]
            cursor -= 1
        result[cursor + 1] = key
        assert result[: end + 1] == sorted(result[: end + 1])
    return result
